$comment: W7: This oneOf is a documented bridge between the Python (type+data) and Rust (kind+flat) envelope shapes. The two emitters genuinely diverge today; collapsing the union to a single branch (envelope unification) is deferred follow-up work. See docs/architecture/events-schema-contract.md.
title: events.jsonl line (v3)
description: One line of events.jsonl. Validates against either the Python/fno envelope (Branch A) or the Rust/fno-agents supervisor envelope (Branch B).

oneOf.$comment: Branch A: Python/fno envelope. Field: type (not kind). Payload nested under data.

type.description: Event type name from events-schema.yaml event_types

source.description: Producer identity for the Python/fno emitter

oneOf.$comment: Branch B: Rust/fno-agents supervisor envelope. Field: kind (not type). Payload fields flattened at top level.

ts.description: UTC RFC3339 timestamp with milliseconds and Z suffix (e.g. 2026-05-07T09:30:42.123Z)

kind.description: Event kind name from the Rust KNOWN_EVENT_KINDS constant. Closed enum; kept in lockstep with the constant by the cross-language parity gate (compare_json diffs this set against fno-agents --emit-schema).

source.description: Producer identity: 'daemon' for the supervisor daemon, 'worker:<id>' for a per-agent worker process
